task: List every module inside fastapi/ that imports from starlette and report which starlette names each one imports.
Find the file that defines the FastAPI class and report the name of its parent class plus the line number of the class definition.
Locate every Python file under fastapi/security/ and for each one report the public class names it defines.
Identify which file in the fastapi package owns the APIRoute class and list the names of its public methods.
List the dependency-injection helper functions defined in fastapi/dependencies/utils.py and give a one-line description of each.
Write a new APIRoute subclass that follows the constructor signature and attribute conventions used in fastapi/routing.py APIRoute, adding a single new attribute called custom_tag.

Write a new Depends-style helper named DependsCached that mirrors the structure of fastapi/param_functions.py Depends but caches its result by argument tuple. Match the existing param_functions.py style.
Write a small FastAPI security scheme class that follows the inheritance and __call__ conventions of fastapi/security/api_key.py APIKeyHeader, but reads from a query parameter instead.
Write a custom exception handler registration following the exact patterns used in fastapi/exception_handlers.py — same function signature shape, same response construction style.
Write a new request body parameter helper following the structure used in fastapi/params.py Body, adding a parameter called transform that takes a callable applied to the parsed value.
Find a code pattern that is repeated across at least three files in fastapi/ and write a verified Python skill that abstracts it. Cite the three source files.
Synthesize how fastapi handles request validation across params.py, dependencies/utils.py, and routing.py and produce a working minimal reproduction in under 60 lines.
